message.content:
Explore frustum culling and viewport-based rendering optimization patterns for Three.js and React Three Fiber:

1. How to implement frustum culling for InstancedMesh
2. How to filter visible items based on camera viewport
3. Best practices for HTML overlays (drei Html) performance:
   - Should they be limited to visible viewport only?
   - Are there pooling patterns?
   
4. Search the codebase for any existing viewport/bounds calculations that could be used for culling

5. Look at how the current MicroLayer handles TaskLabels - this is likely the main performance issue since it creates many HTML elements

Provide specific optimization strategies with code patterns.

content.text: I'll conduct a comprehensive exploration of frustum culling and viewport-based rendering optimization patterns for this codebase. Let me start by examining the current implementation and then research best practices.

content.content:
Web search results for query: "frustum culling Three.js React Three Fiber 2025"

Links: [{"title":"InstancedMesh2 (frustum culling support for instanced meshes) · Issue #3314 · pmndrs/react-three-fiber","url":"https://github.com/pmndrs/react-three-fiber/issues/3314"},{"title":"Impossible to disable frustum culling (?) · Issue #3949 · mrdoob/three.js","url":"https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js/issues/3949"},{"title":"[Solved] InstancedMesh dissapeared because of frustum - Questions - three.js forum","url":"https://discourse.threejs.org/t/solved-instancedmesh-dissapeared-because-of-frustum/53651"},{"title":"`frustumCulled` prop missing from most `Object3D` except `mesh`s · Issue #1931 · pmndrs/react-three-fiber","url":"https://github.com/pmndrs/react-three-fiber/issues/1931"},{"title":"three.js docs","url":"https://threejs.org/docs/#api/core/Object3D.frustumCulled"},{"title":"Expose objects' frustum culled state · Issue #15339 · mrdoob/three.js","url":"https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js/issues/15339"},{"title":"Speeding Up Three.JS with Depth-Based Fragment Culling - Casey Primozic's Homepage","url":"https://cprimozic.net/blog/depth-based-fragment-culling-webgl/"},{"title":"InstancedMesh2 - Easy handling and frustum culling - Discussion - three.js forum","url":"https://discourse.threejs.org/t/instancedmesh2-easy-handling-and-frustum-culling/58622"},{"title":"How to do frustum culling with instancedMesh? - Questions - three.js forum","url":"https://discourse.threejs.org/t/how-to-do-frustum-culling-with-instancedmesh/22633"},{"title":"Getting Started with React Three Fiber","url":"https://pierre.io/journal/react-three-fiber-guide/"}]

Here are the search results for "frustum culling Three.js React Three Fiber 2025":

## Key Findings

### Frustum Culling in React Three Fiber

`frustumCulled` is a property available for all Object3D instances in Three.js (by default, true) which lets instances avoid culling even if they are outside of the camera frustum; however, there have been issues reported where React Three Fiber was missing this prop on some Object3D types.(no content)React Three Fiber supports frustum culling to avoid rendering off-screen objects, along with other advanced Three.js features like custom shaders and materials, post-processing effects, physics integration with Cannon.js or Rapier, and animation systems with React Spring.

### InstancedMesh Frustum Culling

There's been discussion about integrating InstancedMesh2, a library that allows for well-supported frustum culling among other features. Users have asked if there will be a dedicated component and for examples of how to properly use InstancedMesh2 with React Three Fiber.(no content)InstancedMesh2 is an extension class that extends InstancedMesh and manages an array of InstancedEntity (similar to Object3D) that allows you to manage position, scale, rotation and visibility. Visibility is managed by swapping the element you wish to hide with the last one in the instanceMatrix array and decreasing the count by 1.

### Common Issues

Users have reported issues with InstancedMesh disappearing when camera position moves far (e.g., at -100 and 300 on x or z axis) when using React Three Fiber. The issue doesn't occur with normal, non-instanced meshes no matter how far the camera goes.

### Depth-Based Fragment Culling (Alternative Optimization)

Frustum culling takes the region of the world that the camera can see, intersects it with the bounding boxes of every object in the scene, and skips rendering everything that doesn't intersect with the camera's viewbox (frustum).(no content)Manual depth-based fragment culling (a different optimization technique) has been shown to reduce GPU work by around 20% in Three.js applications.

### Resources

- [InstancedMesh2 library](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@three.ez/instanced-mesh) - npm package for enhanced instanced mesh with frustum culling
- [Three.js Object3D.frustumCulled documentation](https://threejs.org/docs/#api/core/Object3D.frustumCulled)
- [React Three Fiber GitHub issues](https://github.com/pmndrs/react-three-fiber/issues) for ongoing discussions


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Building Efficient Three.js Scenes: Optimize Performance While Maintaining Quality | Codrops","url":"https://tympanus.net/codrops/2025/02/11/building-efficient-three-js-scenes-optimize-performance-while-maintaining-quality/"},{"title":"View - Drei","url":"https://drei.docs.pmnd.rs/portals/view"},{"title":"Performance tips — Three.js Journey","url":"https://threejs-journey.com/lessons/performance-tips"},{"title":"Performance recommendation: use canvas renderer on low zoom for better performance on graphs with 100+ nodes · Issue #5442 · xyflow/xyflow","url":"https://github.com/xyflow/xyflow/issues/5442"},{"title":"Using View from drei decreases performance - Questions - three.js forum","url":"https://discourse.threejs.org/t/using-view-from-drei-decreases-performance/67831"},{"title":"How can I optimise my THREE.JS rendering? - Questions - three.js forum","url":"https://discourse.threejs.org/t/how-can-i-optimise-my-three-js-rendering/42251"},{"title":"Optimizing 3D performance — Godot Engine (stable) documentation in English","url":"https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/optimization/optimizing_3d_performance.html"},{"title":"How to improve performance on my site? - Questions - three.js forum","url":"https://discourse.threejs.org/t/how-to-improve-performance-on-my-site/52039"},{"title":"Html - Drei","url":"https://drei.docs.pmnd.rs/misc/html"},{"title":"Introduction - Drei","url":"https://drei.docs.pmnd.rs/"}]

Here are the search results for "drei Html performance optimization viewport culling 2025":

## Key Results

### Building Efficient Three.js Scenes (Codrops - Feb 2025)
A comprehensive look at how to optimize Three.js scenes using Fiber, Drei, and advanced tools, ensuring smooth performance while retaining high-quality visuals.

### Drei View Component
Views use gl.scissor to cut the viewport into segments. You tie a view to a tracking div which then controls the position and bounds of the viewport. This allows you to have multiple views with a single, performant canvas.(no content)These views will follow their tracking elements, scroll along, resize, etc.(no content)If you know your view is always at the same place, set the `frames` prop to 1 to avoid needless getBoundingClientRect overhead.

### Drei Html Component
The Html component allows you to tie HTML content to any object of your scene. It will be projected to the objects whereabouts automatically. Html can hide behind geometry using the occlude prop.

### Viewport Culling Discussion (xyflow - Aug 2025)
Another very important topic is culling - hiding HTML elements and not drawing canvas shapes outside the viewport.(no content)If zoomed in, browsers still have to consider the layout of all the transformed layers. This is very unneeded and causes devices to heat even when actually displaying a couple nodes on the screen with a hundred of them outside the viewport.(no content)From gamedev experience, a simple and performant way to do that is to use a spatial hashing data structure (like a quadtree).

### Drei View Performance Issues (Three.js Forum)
There are reported problems with View from drei. When rendering a scene directly onto the canvas like `<Canvas><Scene/></Canvas>` it runs at 144fps, but when using the View component to render the same scene, performance decreases.

Would you like me to search for more specific information on any of these topics?

content.content:
Web search results for query: "InstancedMesh frustum culling performance optimization"

Links: [{"title":"How to do frustum culling with instancedMesh? - Questions - three.js forum","url":"https://discourse.threejs.org/t/how-to-do-frustum-culling-with-instancedmesh/22633"},{"title":"GitHub - agargaro/instanced-mesh: Enhanced InstancedMesh with frustum culling, fast raycasting (using a BVH), sorting, visibility, LOD, skinning and more.","url":"https://github.com/agargaro/instanced-mesh"},{"title":"Ideas on performing fast per-instance frustum culling on InstancedMesh - Questions - three.js forum","url":"https://discourse.threejs.org/t/ideas-on-performing-fast-per-instance-frustum-culling-on-instancedmesh/85156"},{"title":"InstancedMesh2 - Easy handling and frustum culling - Discussion - three.js forum","url":"https://discourse.threejs.org/t/instancedmesh2-easy-handling-and-frustum-culling/58622"},{"title":"VR Me Up - Three.js InstancedMesh Performance Optimizations","url":"https://vrmeup.com/devlog/devlog_10_threejs_instancedmesh_performance_optimizations.html"},{"title":"Frustum Culling | InstancedMesh2","url":"https://agargaro.github.io/instanced-mesh/basics/06-frustum-culling/"},{"title":"Problem implementing frustum culling and sorting for InstancedMesh · Issue #28102 · mrdoob/three.js","url":"https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js/issues/28102"},{"title":"InstancedMesh: Proposal to support sorting, frustum culling · Issue #27170 · mrdoob/three.js","url":"https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js/issues/27170"},{"title":"@three.ez/instanced-mesh - npm","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/@three.ez/instanced-mesh?activeTab=readme"},{"title":"Three.ez/InstancedMesh2 - Enhanced InstancedMesh with frustum culling, fast raycasting (BVH), sorting, visibility management, LOD, skinning and more - Resources - three.js forum","url":"https://discourse.threejs.org/t/three-ez-instancedmesh2-enhanced-instancedmesh-with-frustum-culling-fast-raycasting-bvh-sorting-visibility-management-lod-skinning-and-more/69344"}]

Based on my search results, here's what I found about InstancedMesh frustum culling performance optimization:

## Key Findings

### The Problem with Standard InstancedMesh
Frustum culling for individual instances is not supported natively by three.js.(no content)In instanced mode, all the meshes are sent to the GPU. Even though the GPU may not render the entire mesh, as it may be outside its triangle view frustum culling step, the mesh still has to go through the vertex shader. On devices with a low end GPU, this can actually decrease performance.

### Common Solution Approach
To solve this, allocate space for a maximum number of instances when creating the Instanced Mesh but set the count to only render the number of instances that are visible. On each frame, iterate over the instances and move the visible ones to the front of the instance array and the ones outside the view frustum to the hidden section and reset the count.

### InstancedMesh2 Library
InstancedMesh2 is an alternative version of InstancedMesh with enhanced features for performance and usability. Key features include:

- Per-instance frustum culling: skip rendering for out-of-view instances.
- Spatial indexing (dynamic BVH): speed up raycasting and frustum culling.
- Level of Detail (LOD): dynamically adjust instance detail based on distance.

### Two Frustum Culling Methods
Frustum culling is performed every frame in two ways: Linear (default): Iterates through all instances, checking if their boundingSphere is inside the camera's frustum. Best for dynamic scenarios. BVH: If a BVH is built, its nodes are recursely iterated. If a node is outside the camera's frustum, the node and all its children are discarded. Best for mostly static scenarios.

### BVH Optimization
To speed up raycasting and frustum culling, a spatial indexing data structure can be created to contain the boundingBoxes of all instances. This works very well if the instances are mostly static (updating a BVH can be expensive) and scattered in world space. Setting a margin makes BVH updating faster, but may make raycasting and frustum culling slightly slower.

### When NOT to Use Frustum Culling
Sometimes, frustum culling can be more costly than beneficial, so it's better to skip it if: Most instances are always within the camera's frustum. The geometry is too simple (e.g., cubes, blades of grass, etc.).

### Combined LOD + Frustum Culling Results
By optimizing view frustum culling and implementing Level of Detail techniques with multiple InstancedMesh objects, the update nearly doubles the frame rate while increasing world complexity and viewing distance.

### Resources
- **InstancedMesh2 library**: Available via npm as `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` ([GitHub](https://github.com/agargaro/instanced-mesh))
- With the current InstancedMesh implementation it's not possible to easily or quickly sort individual instances to improve opaque overdraw for performance or transparency sorting. Similar to how the WebGL_multi_draw extension is used for BatchedMesh, there are "instanced" variants of the multidraw functions that allow for sorting the order in which instanced draws are performed while retaining a single draw call. If desired we could switch InstancedMesh over to use the multidraw extension, as well, and provide support for sorting objects within a single InstancedMesh.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

results:
Based on my search results, here's what I found about InstancedMesh frustum culling performance optimization:

## Key Findings

### The Problem with Standard InstancedMesh
Frustum culling for individual instances is not supported natively by three.js.(no content)In instanced mode, all the meshes are sent to the GPU. Even though the GPU may not render the entire mesh, as it may be outside its triangle view frustum culling step, the mesh still has to go through the vertex shader. On devices with a low end GPU, this can actually decrease performance.

### Common Solution Approach
To solve this, allocate space for a maximum number of instances when creating the Instanced Mesh but set the count to only render the number of instances that are visible. On each frame, iterate over the instances and move the visible ones to the front of the instance array and the ones outside the view frustum to the hidden section and reset the count.

### InstancedMesh2 Library
InstancedMesh2 is an alternative version of InstancedMesh with enhanced features for performance and usability. Key features include:

- Per-instance frustum culling: skip rendering for out-of-view instances.
- Spatial indexing (dynamic BVH): speed up raycasting and frustum culling.
- Level of Detail (LOD): dynamically adjust instance detail based on distance.

### Two Frustum Culling Methods
Frustum culling is performed every frame in two ways: Linear (default): Iterates through all instances, checking if their boundingSphere is inside the camera's frustum. Best for dynamic scenarios. BVH: If a BVH is built, its nodes are recursely iterated. If a node is outside the camera's frustum, the node and all its children are discarded. Best for mostly static scenarios.

### BVH Optimization
To speed up raycasting and frustum culling, a spatial indexing data structure can be created to contain the boundingBoxes of all instances. This works very well if the instances are mostly static (updating a BVH can be expensive) and scattered in world space. Setting a margin makes BVH updating faster, but may make raycasting and frustum culling slightly slower.

### When NOT to Use Frustum Culling
Sometimes, frustum culling can be more costly than beneficial, so it's better to skip it if: Most instances are always within the camera's frustum. The geometry is too simple (e.g., cubes, blades of grass, etc.).

### Combined LOD + Frustum Culling Results
By optimizing view frustum culling and implementing Level of Detail techniques with multiple InstancedMesh objects, the update nearly doubles the frame rate while increasing world complexity and viewing distance.

### Resources
- **InstancedMesh2 library**: Available via npm as `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` ([GitHub](https://github.com/agargaro/instanced-mesh))
- With the current InstancedMesh implementation it's not possible to easily or quickly sort individual instances to improve opaque overdraw for performance or transparency sorting. Similar to how the WebGL_multi_draw extension is used for BatchedMesh, there are "instanced" variants of the multidraw functions that allow for sorting the order in which instanced draws are performed while retaining a single draw call. If desired we could switch InstancedMesh over to use the multidraw extension, as well, and provide support for sorting objects within a single InstancedMesh.
